Review the Quickshell helper migration for correctness, safety, performance, and adherence to 'do not delete anything'. Focus on staged changes in home_manager/hyprland/quickshell.nix, quickshell/helpers/, quickshell/top-bar/scripts/, top-level compatibility wrapper scripts, StatusData.qml helper integration, and waybar.nix conditional legacy package installation. Do not edit files. Report only concrete issues, ordered by severity, with file/line references; state if no blocking issues.

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: checked
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Criteria:
- criterion-1: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

Required evidence: changed-files, tests-added, commands-run, residual-risks, no-staged-files

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
`criteriaSatisfied[].status` must be exactly one of: satisfied, not-satisfied, not-applicable.
`commandsRun[].result` must be exactly one of: passed, failed, not-run.
`manualNotes` and `notes` are optional strings; an empty string means no note and does not satisfy `manual-notes` evidence.
```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "specific proof"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    "src/file.ts"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [
    "test/file.test.ts"
  ],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "command",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "short result"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "validation output or concise summary"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "none"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "short description of the diff",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: file.ts:12 - issue found, or no blockers"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "anything else the parent should know"
}
```

' '--- helpers/default.nix ---'; git show :home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/helpers/default.nix | nl -ba; printf '%s
' '--- update-checker.sh ---'; git show :home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/helpers/update-checker.sh | nl -ba; printf '%s
' '--- update-installer.sh ---'; git show :home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/helpers/update-installer.sh | nl -ba
for f in home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/scripts/*.sh; do printf '\n--- %s ---\n' "$f"; git show ":$f" | nl -ba; done

for f in home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/bluetooth-devices.sh home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/system-stats.sh home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/wifi-connect-password.sh home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/wifi-networks.sh; do printf '
--- %s staged ---
' "$f"; git show ":$f" | nl -ba; printf '%s
' '--- diff ---'; git diff --cached -- "$f"; done

printf '%s\n' '--- StatusData staged ---'; git show :home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/StatusData.qml | nl -ba; printf '%s\n' '--- StatusData diff ---'; git diff --cached -- home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/StatusData.qml

--- helpers/default.nix ---
     1	{pkgs, ...}: let
     2	  gpuUsage = pkgs.rustPlatform.buildRustPackage {
     3	    pname = "gpu-usage-waybar";
     4	    version = "v0.1.24";
     5	
     6	    src = pkgs.fetchFromGitHub {
     7	      owner = "PolpOnline";
     8	      repo = "gpu-usage-waybar";
     9	      rev = "v0.1.23";
    10	      hash = "sha256-DUIKiUgTy4jn8NZZvjC0zuA993Sbq1Fvr7tvJw3+tNw=";
    11	    };
    12	
    13	    cargoHash = "sha256-X3Ak0K1kt7++tE7qZgy8GaRzqemUNTJ3z1yGBJZyA4s=";
    14	    doCheck = false;
    15	  };
    16	
    17	  updateChecker = pkgs.writeShellApplication {
    18	    name = "quickshell-update-checker";
    19	    runtimeInputs = with pkgs; [
    20	      coreutils
    21	      gawk
    22	      gnugrep
    23	      jq
    24	      nix
    25	      util-linux
    26	    ];
    27	    text = builtins.readFile ./update-checker.sh;
    28	  };
    29	
    30	  updateInstaller = pkgs.writeShellApplication {
    31	    name = "quickshell-update-installer";
    32	    runtimeInputs = with pkgs; [
    33	      coreutils
    34	      jq
    35	      nh
    36	      nix
    37	      quickshell
    38	    ];
    39	    text = builtins.readFile ./update-installer.sh;
    40	  };
    41	
    42	  gpuMonitor = pkgs.writeShellApplication {
    43	    name = "quickshell-gpu-monitor";
    44	    text = ''
    45	      export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/run/opengl-driver/lib
    46	      exec ${gpuUsage}/bin/gpu-usage-waybar "$@"
    47	    '';
    48	  };
    49	
    50	  weather = pkgs.writeShellApplication {
    51	    name = "quickshell-weather";
    52	    runtimeInputs = [pkgs.wttrbar];
    53	    text = ''
    54	      exec wttrbar --nerd "$@"
    55	    '';
    56	  };
    57	in {
    58	  home.packages = [
    59	    updateChecker
    60	    updateInstaller
    61	    gpuMonitor
    62	    weather
    63	    pkgs.jq
    64	  ];
    65	}
--- update-checker.sh ---
     1	set -euo pipefail
     2	
     3	flake_dir="${NIXOS_FLAKE_DIR:-$HOME/.config/nixos}"
     4	cache_dir="${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/quickshell/top-bar"
     5	cache_file="$cache_dir/updates.json"
     6	lock_file="$cache_dir/updates.lock"
     7	max_age=1800
     8	
     9	mkdir -p "$cache_dir"
    10	
    11	write_status() {
    12	  local updates_json=$1
    13	  local message=$2
    14	  local temporary
    15	  temporary=$(mktemp "$cache_dir/updates.XXXXXX")
    16	
    17	  jq -cn \
    18	    --argjson updates "$updates_json" \
    19	    --arg message "$message" \
    20	    '{hasUpdates: ($updates | length > 0), message: $message,
    21	      updates: $updates, checkedAt: (now | floor)}' >"$temporary"
    22	  mv "$temporary" "$cache_file"

--- home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/scripts/bluetooth-devices.sh ---
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -u
     3	
     4	scan=${1:-no}
     5	if [[ "$scan" == "yes" ]]; then
     6	  bluetoothctl --timeout 5 scan on >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
     7	fi
     8	
     9	paired=$(bluetoothctl devices Paired 2>/dev/null || true)
    10	connected=$(bluetoothctl devices Connected 2>/dev/null || true)
    11	
    12	bluetoothctl devices 2>/dev/null \
    13	  | while IFS= read -r line; do
    14	      [[ "$line" == Device\ * ]] || continue
    15	      device=${line#Device }
    16	      address=${device%% *}
    17	      name=${device#* }
    18	      [[ "$name" == "$address" ]] && name="Unknown device"
    19	
    20	      is_paired=false
    21	      is_connected=false
    22	      if grep -Fq -- "Device $address " <<< "$paired"; then
    23	        is_paired=true
    24	      fi
    25	      if grep -Fq -- "Device $address " <<< "$connected"; then
    26	        is_connected=true
    27	      fi
    28	
    29	      jq -cn \
    30	        --arg address "$address" \
    31	        --arg name "$name" \
    32	        --argjson paired "$is_paired" \
    33	        --argjson connected "$is_connected" \
    34	        '{address: $address, name: $name, paired: $paired, connected: $connected}'
    35	    done \
    36	  | jq -sc 'sort_by(if .connected then 0 elif .paired then 1 else 2 end, .name)'

--- home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/scripts/system-stats.sh ---
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -u
     3	
     4	read_cpu() {
     5	  read -r _ user nice system idle iowait irq softirq steal _ < /proc/stat
     6	  CPU_IDLE=$((idle + iowait))
     7	  CPU_TOTAL=$((user + nice + system + idle + iowait + irq + softirq + steal))
     8	}
     9	
    10	cpu=0
    11	memory=0
    12	disk=0
    13	brightness=0
    14	network_type="disconnected"
    15	network_name="Disconnected"
    16	network_strength=0
    17	count=0
    18	
    19	read_cpu
    20	previous_idle=$CPU_IDLE
    21	previous_total=$CPU_TOTAL
    22	
    23	while true; do
    24	  sleep 1
    25	
    26	  read_cpu
    27	  delta_idle=$((CPU_IDLE - previous_idle))
    28	  delta_total=$((CPU_TOTAL - previous_total))
    29	  if ((delta_total > 0)); then
    30	    cpu=$(((100 * (delta_total - delta_idle) + delta_total / 2) / delta_total))
    31	  fi
    32	  previous_idle=$CPU_IDLE
    33	  previous_total=$CPU_TOTAL
    34	
    35	  memory=$(awk '
    36	    /^MemTotal:/ { total = $2 }
    37	    /^MemAvailable:/ { available = $2 }
    38	    END { if (total > 0) printf "%.0f", 100 * (total - available) / total; else print 0 }
    39	  ' /proc/meminfo)
    40	
    41	  brightness=$(brightnessctl -m 2>/dev/null | awk -F, 'NR == 1 { gsub(/%/, "", $4); print $4 }')
    42	  brightness=${brightness:-0}
    43	
    44	  if ((count % 5 == 0)); then
    45	    device_status=$(nmcli -t --escape no -f DEVICE,TYPE,STATE device status 2>/dev/null)
    46	    wifi_device=$(awk -F: '$2 == "wifi" && $3 == "connected" { print $1; exit }' <<< "$device_status")
    47	    ethernet_device=$(awk -F: '$2 == "ethernet" && $3 == "connected" { print $1; exit }' <<< "$device_status")
    48	
    49	    if [[ -n "$wifi_device" ]]; then
    50	      network_type="wifi"
    51	      network_name=$(nmcli -t --escape no -f IN-USE,SSID device wifi list ifname "$wifi_device" 2>/dev/null \
    52	        | awk '$0 ~ /^\*/ { sub(/^\*:/, ""); print; exit }')
    53	      network_name=${network_name:-$wifi_device}
    54	      network_strength=$(nmcli -t --escape no -f IN-USE,SIGNAL device wifi list ifname "$wifi_device" 2>/dev/null \
    55	        | awk -F: '$1 == "*" { print $2; exit }')
    56	      network_strength=${network_strength:-0}
    57	    elif [[ -n "$ethernet_device" ]]; then
    58	      network_type="ethernet"
    59	      network_name="$ethernet_device"
    60	      network_strength=100
    61	    elif [[ $(nmcli radio wifi 2>/dev/null) == "disabled" ]]; then
    62	      network_type="disabled"
    63	      network_name="Wi-Fi Off"
    64	      network_strength=0
    65	    else
    66	      network_type="disconnected"
    67	      network_name="Disconnected"
    68	      network_strength=0
    69	    fi
    70	  fi
    71	
    72	  if ((count % 30 == 0)); then
    73	    disk=$(df -P / 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR == 2 { gsub(/%/, "", $5); print $5 }')
    74	    disk=${disk:-0}
    75	  fi
    76	
    77	  jq -cn \
    78	    --argjson cpu "$cpu" \
    79	    --argjson memory "$memory" \
    80	    --argjson disk "$disk" \
    81	    --argjson brightness "$brightness" \
    82	    --arg networkType "$network_type" \
    83	    --arg networkName "$network_name" \
    84	    --argjson networkStrength "$network_strength" \
    85	    '{cpu: $cpu, memory: $memory, disk: $disk, brightness: $brightness,
    86	      networkType: $networkType, networkName: $networkName,
    87	      networkStrength: $networkStrength}'
    88	
    89	  count=$((count + 1))
    90	done

--- home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/scripts/wifi-connect-password.sh ---
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -u
     3	
     4	ssid=${1:?missing SSID}
     5	IFS= read -r password
     6	
     7	# --ask reads the secret from stdin, keeping it out of Quickshell's process
     8	# command and out of nmcli's command-line arguments.
     9	printf '%s\n' "$password" | nmcli --ask --wait 15 device wifi connect "$ssid"

--- home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/scripts/wifi-networks.sh ---
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -u
     3	
     4	rescan=${1:-auto}
     5	if [[ "$rescan" != "yes" && "$rescan" != "auto" ]]; then
     6	  rescan=auto
     7	fi
     8	
     9	known_wifi=$(nmcli -t --escape no -f NAME,TYPE connection show 2>/dev/null \
    10	  | awk -F: '
    11	      $NF == "802-11-wireless" {
    12	        name = $1
    13	        for (i = 2; i < NF; i++) name = name ":" $i
    14	        print name
    15	      }
    16	    ')
    17	
    18	nmcli -t --escape no -f IN-USE,SIGNAL,SECURITY,SSID device wifi list --rescan "$rescan" 2>/dev/null \
    19	  | awk -F: '
    20	      {
    21	        ssid = $4
    22	        for (i = 5; i <= NF; i++) ssid = ssid ":" $i
    23	        if (ssid == "") next
    24	
    25	        signal = $2 + 0
    26	        is_active = ($1 == "*")
    27	        if (!(ssid in strongest) || signal > strongest[ssid]) {
    28	          strongest[ssid] = signal
    29	          security[ssid] = $3
    30	        }
    31	        if (is_active) active[ssid] = 1
    32	      }
    33	      END {
    34	        for (ssid in strongest)
    35	          printf "%s\t%d\t%s\t%d\n", ssid, strongest[ssid], security[ssid], active[ssid] ? 1 : 0
    36	      }
    37	    ' \
    38	  | while IFS=$'\t' read -r ssid strength security active; do
    39	      known=false
    40	      if grep -Fxq -- "$ssid" <<< "$known_wifi"; then
    41	        known=true
    42	      fi
    43	
    44	      jq -cn \
    45	        --arg ssid "$ssid" \
    46	        --argjson strength "$strength" \
    47	        --arg security "$security" \
    48	        --argjson active "$active" \
    49	        --argjson known "$known" \
    50	        '{ssid: $ssid, strength: $strength, security: $security,
    51	          active: ($active == 1), known: $known}'
    52	    done \
    53	  | jq -sc 'sort_by(if .active then 0 else 1 end, -.strength, .ssid)'

    47	    ethernet_device=$(awk -F: '$2 == "ethernet" && $3 == "connected" { print $1; exit }' <<< "$device_status")
    48	
    49	    if [[ -n "$wifi_device" ]]; then
    50	      network_type="wifi"
    51	      network_name=$(nmcli -t --escape no -f IN-USE,SSID device wifi list ifname "$wifi_device" 2>/dev/null \
    52	        | awk '$0 ~ /^\*/ { sub(/^\*:/, ""); print; exit }')
    53	      network_name=${network_name:-$wifi_device}
    54	      network_strength=$(nmcli -t --escape no -f IN-USE,SIGNAL device wifi list ifname "$wifi_device" 2>/dev/null \
    55	        | awk -F: '$1 == "*" { print $2; exit }')
    56	      network_strength=${network_strength:-0}
    57	    elif [[ -n "$ethernet_device" ]]; then
    58	      network_type="ethernet"
    59	      network_name="$ethernet_device"
    60	      network_strength=100
    61	    elif [[ $(nmcli radio wifi 2>/dev/null) == "disabled" ]]; then
    62	      network_type="disabled"
    63	      network_name="Wi-Fi Off"
    64	      network_strength=0
    65	    else
    66	      network_type="disconnected"
    67	      network_name="Disconnected"
    68	      network_strength=0
    69	    fi
    70	  fi
    71	
    72	  if ((count % 30 == 0)); then
    73	    disk=$(df -P / 2>/dev/null | awk 'NR == 2 { gsub(/%/, "", $5); print $5 }')
    74	    disk=${disk:-0}
    75	  fi
    76	
    77	  jq -cn \
    78	    --argjson cpu "$cpu" \
    79	    --argjson memory "$memory" \
    80	    --argjson disk "$disk" \
    81	    --argjson brightness "$brightness" \
    82	    --arg networkType "$network_type" \
    83	    --arg networkName "$network_name" \
    84	    --argjson networkStrength "$network_strength" \
    85	    '{cpu: $cpu, memory: $memory, disk: $disk, brightness: $brightness,
    86	      networkType: $networkType, networkName: $networkName,
    87	      networkStrength: $networkStrength}'
    88	
    89	  count=$((count + 1))
    90	done

--- home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/scripts/wifi-connect-password.sh ---
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -u
     3	
     4	ssid=${1:?missing SSID}
     5	IFS= read -r password
     6	
     7	# --ask reads the secret from stdin, keeping it out of Quickshell's process
     8	# command and out of nmcli's command-line arguments.
     9	printf '%s\n' "$password" | nmcli --ask --wait 15 device wifi connect "$ssid"

--- home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/top-bar/scripts/wifi-networks.sh ---
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	set -u
     3	
     4	rescan=${1:-auto}
     5	if [[ "$rescan" != "yes" && "$rescan" != "auto" ]]; then
     6	  rescan=auto
     7	fi
     8	
     9	known_wifi=$(nmcli -t --escape no -f NAME,TYPE connection show 2>/dev/null \
    10	  | awk -F: '
    11	      $NF == "802-11-wireless" {
    12	        name = $1
    13	        for (i = 2; i < NF; i++) name = name ":" $i
    14	        print name
    15	      }
    16	    ')
    17	
    18	nmcli -t --escape no -f IN-USE,SIGNAL,SECURITY,SSID device wifi list --rescan "$rescan" 2>/dev/null \
    19	  | awk -F: '
    20	      {
    21	        ssid = $4
    22	        for (i = 5; i <= NF; i++) ssid = ssid ":" $i
    23	        if (ssid == "") next
    24	
    25	        signal = $2 + 0
    26	        is_active = ($1 == "*")
    27	        if (!(ssid in strongest) || signal > strongest[ssid]) {
    28	          strongest[ssid] = signal
    29	          security[ssid] = $3
    30	        }
    31	        if (is_active) active[ssid] = 1
    32	      }
    33	      END {
    34	        for (ssid in strongest)
    35	          printf "%s\t%d\t%s\t%d\n", ssid, strongest[ssid], security[ssid], active[ssid] ? 1 : 0
    36	      }
    37	    ' \
    38	  | while IFS=$'\t' read -r ssid strength security active; do
    39	      known=false
    40	      if grep -Fxq -- "$ssid" <<< "$known_wifi"; then
    41	        known=true
    42	      fi
    43	
    44	      jq -cn \
    45	        --arg ssid "$ssid" \
    46	        --argjson strength "$strength" \
    47	        --arg security "$security" \
    48	        --argjson active "$active" \
    49	        --argjson known "$known" \
    50	        '{ssid: $ssid, strength: $strength, security: $security,
    51	          active: ($active == 1), known: $known}'
    52	    done \
    53	  | jq -sc 'sort_by(if .active then 0 else 1 end, -.strength, .ssid)'

text:
1	# Quickshell layout
     2	
     3	- `top-bar/` — active Quickshell configuration managed by Home Manager.
     4	  - `*.qml` — shell, state and visual components.
     5	  - `scripts/` — maintained runtime scripts used through `Quickshell.shellDir`.
     6	  - top-level `*.sh` files — preserved compatibility entry points forwarding to `scripts/`.
     7	  - `DESIGN_GUIDE.md` — visual and animation conventions for future changes.
     8	- `helpers/` — Nix-packaged commands used by the active shell.
     9	  - `quickshell-update-checker`
    10	  - `quickshell-update-installer`
    11	  - `quickshell-gpu-monitor`
    12	  - `quickshell-weather`
    13	
    14	The old helper definitions remain in `../waybar.nix` for compatibility, but are only installed when Waybar is enabled. Waybar itself remains disabled.

--- top bar shell.qml ---
     1	import Quickshell
     2	
     3	ShellRoot {
     4	  StatusData {
     5	    id: sharedStatus
     6	  }
     7	
     8	  Variants {
     9	    model: Quickshell.screens
    10	
    11	    Bar {
    12	      statusData: sharedStatus
    13	    }
    14	  }
    15	}

--- home manager default ---
     1	{inputs, ...}: {
     2	  imports = [
     3	    inputs.catppuccin.homeModules.catppuccin
     4	    # inputs.hyprpanel.homeManagerModules.hyprpanel
     5	
     6	    ../shared
     7	    ./gtk.nix

arguments.command:
set -euo pipefail
cache=$(mktemp -d)
trap 'rm -rf "$cache"' EXIT
XDG_CACHE_HOME="$cache" NIXOS_FLAKE_DIR="$PWD" timeout 240 quickshell-update-checker force | tee "$cache/result.json" | jq -e 'type == "object" and (.hasUpdates | type == "boolean") and (.message | type == "string") and (.updates | type == "array") and ((.checkedAt | type) == "number")'
printf '%s\n' '--- parsed result ---'
jq . "$cache/result.json"

set -euo pipefail
printf '%s\n' '--- installed quickshell config files ---'
find -L ~/.config/quickshell/top-bar -maxdepth 3 -type f -printf '%P\n' | sort
printf '%s\n' '--- target modes/targets ---'
find -L ~/.config/quickshell/top-bar -maxdepth 2 -type f -name '*.sh' -printf '%m %P\n' | sort

set -euo pipefail
printf '%s\n' '--- helper binary closure checks ---'
for cmd in quickshell-update-checker quickshell-update-installer quickshell-gpu-monitor quickshell-weather; do
  path=$(readlink -f "$(command -v "$cmd")")
  echo "$cmd -> $path"
  nix-store -q --references "$(dirname "$(dirname "$path")")" >/dev/null
done
printf '%s\n' 'helper store paths and closures resolve'

set -euo pipefail
printf '%s\n' '--- staged vs deployed key helper hashes ---'
for f in helpers/update-checker.sh helpers/update-installer.sh top-bar/StatusData.qml top-bar/scripts/system-stats.sh top-bar/system-stats.sh; do
  staged=$(git show ":home_manager/hyprland/quickshell/$f" | sha256sum | cut -d' ' -f1)
  case "$f" in
    helpers/*) echo "$f staged=$staged (packaged script source; no deployed source path)" ;;
    top-bar/*) deployed=$(sha256sum "$HOME/.config/quickshell/${f#top-bar/}" | cut -d' ' -f1); echo "$f staged=$staged deployed=$deployed" ;;
  esac
done
